metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

coverage.verify_error: step 'review@then/verify_branch@then/verify' failed: the LLM provider call failed: Streamed response ended without content or tool calls

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'move 9 sharpened for verifiable-proxy' with a concrete observable outcome, e.g. 'move 9 includes a named verifiable-proxy clause: a template sentence that reframes an unobservable/deferred success target as an in-session-observable proxy, and the registry entry is updated to include this clause'.
findings.finding: AC2 ('move 9 sharpened for verifiable-proxy') uses a subjective effort term ('sharpened') rather than stating a specific observable outcome — an executor cannot know what the move's new language must say or what test would confirm it is sufficient.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'move 9 sharpened for verifiable-proxy'
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC2 as: 'Move 9 registry entry updated to include a verifiable-proxy sub-pattern: a locked template clause stating "Restate [unobservable target] as [in-session-observable proxy] so the AC is evaluable before session close" — visible in the registry file diff.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving check for each AC (e.g. 'registry file contains an entry whose id matches the foundation/enhancement move slug', 'move 9 entry diff shows the verifiable-proxy clause', 'a regression test or doc fixture asserts post-hoc coaching routes to discovered_from, not AC deferral').
findings.finding: None of the three ACs have a concrete proving command or check that produces evidence of completion — there is no stated way to verify that any move was added, that move 9 was updated, or that the routing behavior exists.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sentence to the What section defining what 'locked template' and 'bounded {subject}' mean for the foundation/enhancement move so AC1 is fully traceable to described work.
findings.finding: AC1 introduces a 'locked template' constraint and 'bounded {subject}' requirement that appear nowhere in the What section's description of the foundation/enhancement move, creating an orphaned AC constraint with no corresponding described deliverable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC line 1: 'foundation/enhancement move added (locked template, bounded {subject})'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, append: 'The move template will be locked (no free-form fields other than {subject}), and {subject} must be bounded to a single named feature or deliverable to prevent scope drift.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state whether sharpening move 9 is backward-compatible (additive extension) or a narrowing change, and if the latter, describe the rollback or expand-contract sequencing for existing move 9 consumers.
findings.finding: Move 9 is being 'sharpened' (behavioral change to an existing registry entry) but no rollback path or expand-contract sequencing is described for coaching flows that currently invoke move 9 under its prior semantics.
findings.location: What section, bullet 2: 'Sharpen move 9 (plan verification) to cover restating an unobservable/deferred success target...'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the registry lifecycle for this change: who owns it, how the edit is applied (e.g., single atomic file commit), and whether consumers require an explicit reload or re-derive from source-of-truth on each use.
findings.finding: The move registry is shared/global state but the plan omits the full lifecycle: who owns the registry, how the new move and the sharpened move 9 are applied (migration, file edit, runtime reload), and whether consumers (coaching engine, completion verifier) tolerate the transition or need a coordinated update.
findings.location: ## What — move additions and move 9 sharpening

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and acknowledge any existing consumers of move 9 (coaching engine invocations, agent prompts, documentation) and confirm the sharpened semantics are backward-compatible or that a coordinated update is planned.
findings.finding: Move 9 sharpening changes existing behavior, but the plan does not acknowledge downstream callers/consumers that currently depend on move 9's prior semantics (e.g., coaching sessions already in-flight, agents trained on the old wording, or other plan templates that reference move 9).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify or define the 'scope axis' / 'fidelity axis' taxonomy in the codebase (e.g. in MOVE_REGISTRY annotations or plan-review-gate.md) so the new foundation/enhancement move's distinction from move 7 is grounded, not asserted.
findings.finding: The plan asserts move 7 is on the 'scope axis' as the basis for distinguishing the new foundation/enhancement move ('Distinct from move 7 (scope axis) -- this is the FIDELITY axis'), but the term 'scope axis' does not appear anywhere in the codebase — not in MOVE_REGISTRY, criteria_routing.json, plan-review-gate.md, coach.py, or any other file. Move 7's actual template ('Prove {subject} end-to-end with a thin vertical slice first.') does not self-label as a scope axis. This axis-labelling is an unverifiable characterisation that makes the 'distinctness' argument impossible to ground-check.
findings.location: What section: 'Distinct from move 7 (scope axis) -- this is the FIDELITY axis.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the informal 'F1.in_session_evaluable (BLOCKING)' notation with the accurate codebase term ('F1 criterion, in_session_evaluable checklist key, which fails under F1's blocking posture') so the move 9 sharpening scope is precisely grounded.
findings.finding: The plan describes 'F1.in_session_evaluable (BLOCKING)' using dot-notation and a BLOCKING parenthetical to imply this individual sub-criterion carries its own BLOCKING designation. The actual codebase (criteria_routing.json) shows that BLOCKING is a posture of the *whole* F1 criterion ('default_posture: blocking'), not a per-checklist-key attribute — no sub-criterion key carries a BLOCKING label. The plan's notation is unverifiable as stated and could mislead an executor into believing individual AC sub-keys are individually BLOCKING-designated.
findings.location: What section: 'the verifiable-proxy remediation for an F1.in_session_evaluable block'
findings.suggested_fix: Change to: 'the in_session_evaluable checklist key of the F1 criterion (whose overall default_posture is blocking per criteria_routing.json)' to match the actual data model.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/plan-review-gate.md` (the move registry table, lines 146-160) to the edit targets: update it to reflect both the new foundation/enhancement-split move row and any change to move 9's template string.
findings.finding: `docs/plan-review-gate.md` (lines 146-160) contains a verbatim Markdown table of the full `MOVE_REGISTRY` including every move id, name, and template string. Adding a new foundation/enhancement-split move to `passes.py` AND sharpening move 9's template will both leave this docs table stale, but the plan names no edit target in `docs/plan-review-gate.md`.
findings.location: docs/plan-review-gate.md, lines 146-160 (The Pass-4 move registry table)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or implementation note: 'Update the move registry table in `docs/plan-review-gate.md` (§ "The Pass-4 move registry") to add the new move row and replace move 9's template with the sharpened verifiable-proxy text.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the positive rationale for sharpening move 9's template in-place rather than adding a new targeted move with `applies_when: ["F1"]` narrowing — specifically whether the verifiable-proxy framing is intentionally useful for all verification findings or only for F1.in_session_evaluable blocks.
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for sharpening move 9's template in-place rather than adding a new targeted move with applies_when scoping — a structurally different alternative the codebase already supports and uses in the code-review catalog.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence such as: 'Move 9 is sharpened in-place (not as a new scoped move) because verifiable-proxy restatement is a generally useful verification discipline, not exclusive to F1.in_session_evaluable findings; the broader framing benefits any unobservable success target regardless of which criterion surfaces it.' If the intent is F1-only, instead add a new move (id 10) with `applies_when: ["F1"]` following the code-review catalog pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact locked `template` string for the foundation/enhancement move (must contain exactly one `{subject}` placeholder) so the executor writes verbatim rather than infers.
findings.finding: The locked template string for the new foundation/enhancement move is never defined in the plan. The MOVE_REGISTRY stores {name, template} pairs where `template` is the verbatim, deterministically-rendered coaching prose the LLM never authors — it is the value vocabulary of this move's enum entry. The plan only describes the intent ('Ship a working baseline of {subject} now; make the enhanced version a dependent follow-on') without committing the actual template string an executor would write into passes.py.
findings.location: What section — foundation/enhancement move description; AC1 ('locked template, bounded {subject}')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'Template: "Ship a working baseline of {subject} now; defer the enhanced version as a follow-on ticket."' (or the author's preferred wording), so it is committed in the plan and not invented at implementation time.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Provide the new/updated template string for move 9 (or describe the structural change — e.g. replace vs. add a second entry) so AC2 has an observable, text-grounded definition of done.
findings.finding: Move 9's updated (sharpened) template text is not specified. Currently the template reads: 'Plan how {subject} will be verified before implementing it.' The plan says to sharpen it to cover 'restating an unobservable/deferred success target as an in-session-observable one (the verifiable-proxy remediation for an F1.in_session_evaluable block).' But the new template string is never given. Since the template IS the deterministically-rendered output the LLM coach emits, an executor cannot implement AC2 without inventing the text.
findings.location: What section — 'Sharpen move 9 (plan verification)'; AC2 ('move 9 sharpened for verifiable-proxy')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly decide whether the foundation/enhancement move should have `applies_when: ["F1"]` (or similar trigger tag) or be always-applicable, and state this in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify whether the new foundation/enhancement move should carry an `applies_when` field scoping it to F1 findings. The `applies_when` schema field is the decision rule controlling which moves the LLM is OFFERED — missing it means the move is always-applicable. The plan explicitly ties this move to F1.in_session_evaluable remediation; the code-review gate demonstrates using `applies_when` to scope targeted moves (e.g. 'security', 'db-migrations'). The plan-review gate currently sets no `applies_when` on any move (all always-applicable), but the new move has a narrower, criterion-specific use case that warrants an explicit decision.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit task to update the `docs/plan-review-gate.md` move-registry table (lines 148-160) with the new move entry and the updated move 9 template string.
findings.finding: `docs/plan-review-gate.md` lines 148-160 contains a verbatim Markdown mirror table of the full `MOVE_REGISTRY` (every move id, name, and template string). This table is a second authoritative location that must be updated alongside `passes.py` when moves are added or changed. The plan mentions only the code change (the move registry) but does not mention updating this doc table — creating an instruction-locality gap where the schema and its co-located documentation diverge after the ticket is executed.
findings.location: docs/plan-review-gate.md lines 141-166 ('The Pass-4 move registry' section)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'Update the move-registry table in docs/plan-review-gate.md to reflect the new move and the updated move 9 template.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
